headlines.description: — The Atlanta Falcons and Carolina Panthers have more in common than frustrating streaks of seven consecutive losing seasons.
headlines.shortLinkText: Bryce Young accounts for 5 TDs as Panthers beat Falcons 44-38 in OT. Atlanta misses playoffs again

headlines.description: — Jayden Daniels sitting in the second half wasn’t the plan when the playoff-bound Washington Commanders visited the Dallas Cowboys with only seeding at stake.
headlines.shortLinkText: Mariota leads playoff-bound Commanders to 23-19 win over Cowboys after replacing Daniels

events.name: Chicago Bears at Green Bay Packers

headlines.description: — Caleb Williams gained a shot of confidence and Cairo Santos earned a measure of redemption.
headlines.shortLinkText: Bears rally for 24-22 win in Green Bay as Packers lose Love and Watson to injuries

headlines.description: — The Houston Texans gave what coach DeMeco Ryans wanted from their trip to Music City.
headlines.shortLinkText: Playoff-bound Texans cruise to 23-14 win over the Titans, who land No. 1 draft pick

headlines.description: — Jonathan Taylor closed the season with a flourish. Joe Flacco may have walked off the field for the final time as a winner.
headlines.shortLinkText: Jonathan Taylor and Joe Flacco lead Colts to 26-23 overtime win over Jaguars

events.name: Buffalo Bills at New England Patriots

headlines.description: — The New England Patriots concluded a bitter season on a high note.
headlines.shortLinkText: Patriots beat Bills 23-16 to cost themselves top draft pick. Coach Jerod Mayo fired after game

events.name: New York Giants at Philadelphia Eagles

headlines.description: — Saquon Barkley traded his No. 26 jersey for a T-shirt that celebrated his 2,000 yards rushing this season and Jalen Hurts remained sidelined with a concussion.
headlines.shortLinkText: Barkley and Hurts sit as the Eagles tune up for the playoffs with a 20-13 win over the Giants

events.name: New Orleans Saints at Tampa Bay Buccaneers

headlines.description: — Just 36 seconds away from clinching the NFC South title, the Tampa Bay Buccaneers had another goal to achieve.
headlines.shortLinkText: Buccaneers beat Saints 27-19 to clinch NFC South and wide receiver Mike Evans surpasses 1,000 yards

events.name: Kansas City Chiefs at Denver Broncos

headlines.description: — Bo Nix celebrated the end of Denver's eight-year playoff drought with a bunch of new friends.
headlines.shortLinkText: Nix leads Broncos past Chiefs' reserves 38-0 and into playoffs for 1st time since 2015 season

events.name: Los Angeles Chargers at Las Vegas Raiders

headlines.description: — Chargers quarterback Justin Herbert said he could tell from the noise his teammates made from neighboring meeting rooms on Saturday “that good things were happening.”
headlines.shortLinkText: Justin Herbert and Chargers secure AFC's No. 5 seed with 34-20 win over Raiders

events.name: Seattle Seahawks at Los Angeles Rams

headlines.description: — Matthew Stafford and nearly all of his fellow offensive starters watched the Los Angeles Rams' regular-season finale in street clothes. Coach Sean McVay knew his NFC West champions would fall from the third to the fourth playoff seed if they lost to...
headlines.shortLinkText: Geno Smith throws career-high 4 TD passes in Seahawks' 30-25 win over playoff-bound Rams

events.name: Miami Dolphins at New York Jets

headlines.description: — Aaron Rodgers walked off the field with his buddy Davante Adams by his side, waved his left hand to the New York Jets fans cheering his name in the MetLife Stadium stands and walked through the dark tunnel to the locker room.
headlines.shortLinkText: Rodgers throws a season-high 4 touchdown passes in possible final game as Jets top Dolphins 32-20

headlines.description: — The Cardinals' final passing touchdown of the season was a perfectly placed ball from Kyler Murray to rookie Marvin Harrison Jr. — a 12-yard strike in the fourth quarter that sealed their 47-24 win over the San Francisco 49ers on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Kyler Murray throws 4 TD passes and the Cardinals beat the 49ers 47-24 in finale

headlines.description: — Jahmyr Gibbs was the game-breaking player the Detroit Lions needed to get their high-powered offense in gear — and give them a first-round bye and home-field advantage in the NFC playoffs.
headlines.shortLinkText: Lions beat Vikings 31-9, win NFC North and No. 1 seed, dropping division rivals to No. 5
